methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems

data.narrative:
Frankenburg Technologies is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company that has raised €40M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 private peers and 2 public companies operating in the same sector and stage cohort, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

The peer-median method, applied across three same-sector, same-stage comparables, yields a median valuation of $300M, with observed peer valuations ranging from $150M (Ares Industries) to $500M (Stark Defence). This method represents the primary triangulation input given data availability. The median-based implied valuation of $300M reflects the central tendency of the identified peer set.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $255M (low) to $345M (high), centered on a median of $300M. This range incorporates modest adjustments around the peer-median benchmark to account for stage positioning within Series A. Key uncertainties include the limited single-method triangulation (peer-median only), reliance on a three-company comparable set, and the absence of revenue or profitability multiples to validate relative positioning. Additional data on capital intensity, customer concentration, and go-to-market efficiency would strengthen conviction in placement within the identified range.
